instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What Is the Cash Value of Life Insurance & How To Calculate It?
A life insurance policy can help you secure the financial future of your family members, even if anything unfortunate were to happen to you. However, there may be certain situations where you may need to surrender the policy before it matures. This is where knowing about the surrender value of your life insurance plan becomes essential.
Surrender Value and Associated Charges
Whether you’re tired of paying high premiums or you no longer have beneficiaries who need the death benefit from your plan, you need to know the cash surrender value of your life insurance policy before terminating it.
Cash surrender value refers to the amount you will receive from your insurer when you cancel a plan before it matures. In other words, if you terminate your policy mid-term, you may be eligible for a certain payout amount against the premiums you’ve paid. This depends up on the plan you have opted for and its underlying terms and conditions. Additionally, as per IRDAI (Unit Linked Insurance Products) Regulations, 2019, in case of a ULIP if you choose to terminate the policy after a lock in period of 5 years, no surrender charges can be imposed on it by your insurance provider. However, in case of other products, charges will be applicable.
If you’re wondering how this surrender value is calculated, fret not, for we’ve got you covered! In this article, we will understand the process of calculating the surrender value of a life insurance plan as well as the alternatives to surrendering your policy.
How Is Cash Surrender Value Calculated?
Calculating the surrender value of your insurance plan is quite simple. All you need to do is add the total premiums paid and subtract the charges levied by your insurer for surrendering the plan mid-term.
However, this is not a blanket calculation process. Before you can calculate the surrender value of your life insurance policy, you must know the difference between guaranteed surrender value and special surrender value, as the cash surrender value of your policy will be hinged on these two key concepts when it comes to traditional life insurance plans. For ULIPs, a discontinuance charge will apply between the first to fifth years.
Surrender Values and How To Calculate Them
Guaranteed Surrender Value: This is the amount that is guaranteed to the policyholder in case there is a voluntary termination of the policy by the policyholder prior to the maturity of the policy. You can claim this surrender value after 3 years of paying your insurance premiums.
Alternatives to Surrendering Your Life Insurance Plan
While knowing the cash surrender value of your policy is essential, surrendering it may not always be prudent course of action when in need of cash.
Alternatively, if you have an endowment plan, you can also borrow money against the said policy after you’ve paid premiums for at least 3 years. ULIPs, offer a partial withdrawal option for policyholders. While the rules of borrowing may be different for different insurance providers, generally you may borrow up to 90% of your policy’s cash value. This may help you meet your immediate cash requirements without losing out on the policy’s coverage and death benefits.
When Does a Life Insurance Policy Acquire A Surrender Value?
The surrender value in a life insurance policy depends on the type of policy that you choose. Have a look –
Pure Term Insurance Plans:
Under pure term insurance plans, coverage is provided only for premature demise. There’s no maturity value. As such, these plans don’t have a savings element. Under these plans, there’s no surrender value. If you stop paying premiums and surrender the policy, the coverage will be terminated without any surrender value payment.
Term with the Return of Premium Plans:
Under these types of term plans, the premiums are refunded on maturity. Thus, these plans have a savings element and the provision for a surrender value. The plan acquires a surrender value if premiums for the first two or three years is paid, depending on the policy’s terms and conditions. In the case of single premium plans, the policy acquires a surrender value immediately.
Unit Linked Insurance Plans:
ULIPs have a lock-in period of five years. They acquire a surrender value only after the lock-in period has expired. If you surrender the policy before the completion of the lock-in period, the fund value is transferred to the discontinued policy fund, where it remains for the rest of the lock-in period. After the period is over, the fund value as on the date in the discontinued policy fund is paid as the surrender value.
Other Insurance Plans:
Under other life insurance plans, like endowment, money back, etc., the policy acquires a surrender value after two or three years’ premiums have been paid, depending on the plan chosen. Under single premium plans, the surrender value is available immediately after the policy is issued since the entire premium has been paid.
Conclusion
You can calculate the cash surrender value of your policy with the help of an online life insurance payout calculator. However, by surrendering your policy, you may lose out on sizable benefits since the amount you receive will be lower than the premiums you’ve already paid. Thus, in such cases, looking into alternatives like loans against policy depending on the rules of borrowing of the insurers or partial withdrawals in case of ULIPs may be more prudent options than simple terminations.
References
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/content/dam/balic/pdf/term-insurance/etouch-plan-sl.pdf
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/content/dam/balic/pdf/ulip/goal-assure-brochure.pdf
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/content/dam/balic/pdf/savings-plans/guaranteed-income-goal.pdf
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/content/dam/balic/pdf/savings-plans/guaranteed-saving-goal.pdf
https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/definition/surrender-value#:~:text=A%20regular%20premium%20policy%20acquires,premiums%20continuously%20for%20three%20years.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
